title: The 10 Best Herbal Teas for Osteoarthritis Relief
intro_desc: Herbal teas for osteoarthritis are a natural remedy that helps alleviate the symptoms of this chronic condition by reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving joint mobility. The best herbal teas for osteoarthritis include Curcuma longa, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Equisetum arvense, Rheum palmatum, Ginkgo biloba, Achillea millefolium, Vaccinium myrtillus, Cinchona officinalis, and Boswellia serrata. If left untreated, osteoarthritis can severely limit daily activities, causing chronic pain, stiffness, and limited mobility, ultimately affecting one's quality of life, independence, and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tea, also known as turmeric tea, is a natural remedy that can help with osteoarthritis pain. The main ingredient, curcumin, is a powerful anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling and stiffness in the joints. This can help people with osteoarthritis move more easily and comfortably. Curcumin also helps to reduce the production of certain chemicals in the body that cause pain. Additionally, it has antioxidant properties that protect the joints from damage caused by free radicals. When taken regularly, curcumin may slow down the progression of osteoarthritis and improve joint function. Some studies have shown that curcumin can be as effective as certain medications in reducing osteoarthritis pain. Drinking curcuma longa tea regularly may help to reduce the need for pain medication and improve quality of life for people with osteoarthritis.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for osteoarthritis because it reduces joint pain and inflammation by blocking the production of chemicals that cause swelling and pain in the joints.

Demethoxycurcumin is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, making it a potential natural treatment for people with this condition.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, making it a potential natural remedy for managing osteoarthritis symptoms.

remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal teas for osteoarthritis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal teas that help with osteoarthritis, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Capsicum annuum, also known as dried red pepper or cayenne pepper, and a carrier herb like peppermint or ginger. Next, measure one teaspoon of dried Capsicum annuum and mix it with one teaspoon of the carrier herb. Place the mixture in a cup and add one cup of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. Once the mixture has cooled, strain the liquid into another cup and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon juice to taste, but be cautious not to add too much as the Capsicum annuum has a strong, spicy flavor. To enhance the benefits, consider drinking one to two cups of this herbal tea per day, ideally after meals or before bedtime, to help reduce inflammation and alleviate osteoarthritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a natural remedy that may help with osteoarthritis symptoms. Osteoarthritis is a condition where the joints become inflamed and painful due to wear and tear. Ginger tea contains a compound called gingerol, which has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the joints. When you drink ginger tea, the gingerol is absorbed into the body and travels to the joints, where it helps to reduce inflammation. This can make it easier to move and perform daily activities. Additionally, ginger tea may also help to reduce nausea and improve digestion, which can be a problem for people with osteoarthritis who may have trouble eating due to joint pain. Drinking ginger tea regularly may also help to reduce the risk of further joint damage.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in joints.
Shogaol is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Polyphenols are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and inflammation, and also protect cartilage by preventing oxidative damage and promoting the growth of healthy new tissue.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for osteoarthritis
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal tea for osteoarthritis relief, start by gathering dried red or green cayenne pepper pods, also known as Capsicum annuum, from a local health food store or online. Rinse the pods under running water, then chop them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties. In a large pot, combine 1 teaspoon of the chopped Capsicum annuum with 1 cup of boiling water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds like capsaicin to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids, and add honey or lemon to taste if needed. Drink this tea 2-3 times a week to help alleviate osteoarthritis symptoms, such as joint pain and inflammation, by reducing the production of prostaglandins and other inflammatory chemicals in the body. It's essential to note that while Capsicum annuum tea may provide relief, it's crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for osteoarthritis, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tea, also known as licorice root tea, is a natural remedy that may help with osteoarthritis. Osteoarthritis is a condition where the joints become inflamed and painful due to wear and tear. Licorice root tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling. It also contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have anti-arthritic effects by inhibiting the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body. Additionally, licorice root tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the joints from damage caused by free radicals. Some studies have suggested that licorice root tea may also help to reduce the levels of certain enzymes that break down cartilage, which can contribute to joint damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting the joints, licorice root tea may help to alleviate the symptoms of osteoarthritis. It can be consumed as a warm tea, or added to other herbal teas, making it a potentially useful natural remedy for people with osteoarthritis.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, making it easier for people with osteoarthritis to move and perform daily activities.

Polyphenols are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation in the body.
Flavonoids are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, and also promote the growth of healthy cartilage tissue.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal teas that help with osteoarthritis, start by gathering fresh or dried Capsicum annuum peppers, also known as sweet peppers or bell peppers. You can use any variety, but sweet peppers are preferred for their mild flavor and less heat. Next, dry or chop the peppers and add 1-2 teaspoons of them to a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the peppers and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's essential to note that Capsicum annuum contains capsaicin, which may irritate the digestive system if consumed in excess. For osteoarthritis relief, drink 1-2 cups of this tea per day, ideally after meals, as the capsaicin may help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the joints.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea is a popular remedy for people with osteoarthritis. Osteoarthritis is a condition where the cartilage that cushions joints wears away, causing pain and stiffness. Ginkgo biloba tea contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce joint pain and inflammation. It also improves blood flow to the joints, allowing more oxygen and nutrients to reach the damaged cartilage. This can help to slow down the progression of osteoarthritis and reduce symptoms like stiffness and swelling. Additionally, ginkgo biloba tea has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the joints from damage caused by free radicals. Some studies suggest that ginkgo biloba tea may also help to improve joint mobility and reduce the need for pain medication.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, and also protect cartilage from damage.
Bilobalide is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, making it easier to move and live with the condition.
Isorhapontigenin is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in joints, thus improving overall mobility and quality of life.
Kaempferol is good for osteoarthritis because it helps reduce inflammation and pain in the joints by blocking the action of enzymes that cause inflammation and damage to cartilage tissue.
Ginkgolide B is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain in joints, and also help to slow down the progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal tea that helps with osteoarthritis, start by gathering a few dried or fresh cayenne pepper pods, also known as Capsicum annuum. You can use 1-2 teaspoons of dried pods or 2-3 fresh pods for every 1 cup of boiling water. Next, boil water in a pot and pour it over the cayenne pepper pods in a teapot or mug. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on your preference for strength. Strain the tea into another cup, discarding the solids, and add a bit of honey if you want a sweeter taste. Capsicum annuum contains capsaicin, which helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis, so be sure to consume the tea after a meal to avoid stomach upset. It's recommended to consult a doctor before making any herbal tea a regular part of your treatment plan, as they can help you determine the best way to use Capsicum annuum tea to manage your osteoarthritis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum palmatum tea is a type of herbal tea made from the roots of the plant. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to help with pain and inflammation. The tea contains compounds called glycosides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and ease pain in joints affected by osteoarthritis. The tea may also help slow down the progression of the disease by reducing the wear and tear on joints. Additionally, Rheum palmatum tea has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Some people find that taking Rheum palmatum tea helps them move more easily and with less pain, which can improve their quality of life.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for osteoarthritis because they reduce inflammation and pain by blocking the production of an enzyme called COX-2, which is involved in the formation of chemicals that cause joint pain and swelling.
Flavonoids are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in joints, making it easier to move and perform daily activities.
Tannins are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, and also contain antioxidants that protect the cartilage from damage.

Saponins are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce joint pain and swelling by blocking the production of enzymes that cause cartilage damage.
Rhein is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce joint pain and swelling, making it a potential natural remedy for relieving symptoms of this condition.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Capsicum annuum herbal tea that can help with osteoarthritis, first start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need 1 cup of dried Capsicum annuum peppers, 2 cups of boiling water, and a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup. Begin by placing the dried peppers into the tea infuser or directly into the cup, then pour the boiling water over them. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to release into the water. Once the steeping time is complete, carefully pour the tea into a cup and let it cool down to a comfortable temperature. Strain the tea before consumption if you used loose peppers in the cup. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally before meals, as it may help reduce joint inflammation and relieve pain associated with osteoarthritis. However, please note that you should consult with a healthcare professional before using this tea as a treatment for any medical condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza tea, also known as Danshen tea, is a herbal remedy that may help people with osteoarthritis. This is because the tea contains compounds that can reduce inflammation and pain in the joints. Osteoarthritis is a condition where the joints become stiff and painful due to wear and tear on the cartilage. Salvia miltiorrhiza tea may help to reduce this inflammation and pain by improving blood flow to the affected areas. This can help to reduce stiffness and improve joint mobility. Additionally, the tea may also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the progression of osteoarthritis. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Salvia miltiorrhiza tea may also help to protect the joints from further damage.

Rosmarinic acid is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce pain and swelling in the joints, and also help prevent further damage to the cartilage.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal tea that can help with osteoarthritis, start by gathering fresh or dried Capsicum annuum leaves, also known as sweet peppers or bell peppers. Rinse the leaves with clean water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, combine 1 teaspoon of the leaves with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves, and discard the solids. Finally, add honey or lemon to taste, and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate osteoarthritis symptoms, such as joint pain and inflammation. It's essential to note that while Capsicum annuum has potential anti-inflammatory properties, consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for osteoarthritis, especially if you're already taking medication or have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, has been used for centuries to help people with osteoarthritis. The tea is made from the leaves and flowers of the yarrow plant. It has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce swelling and pain in joints. Osteoarthritis is a condition where the cartilage in joints wears away, causing pain and stiffness. The anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow tea, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, can help to reduce the inflammation and pain in the joints. This can make it easier to move and perform daily activities. The tea also has antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the joints from further damage. Some studies have shown that yarrow tea may also help to reduce the production of certain enzymes that break down cartilage, which can slow down the progression of osteoarthritis. Drinking yarrow tea regularly may help to reduce the symptoms of osteoarthritis and improve joint health.

Kaempferol is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling by blocking the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal teas that help with osteoarthritis, start by gathering dried or fresh Capsicum annuum peppers, also known as cayenne peppers. If using fresh peppers, dry them in a low-temperature oven or a food dehydrator until they are crispy. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the dried or crushed peppers with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a mug. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the liquid using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth into a separate container. Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. The Capsicum annuum tea is said to contain capsaicin, a compound that helps reduce pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally before meals, to experience its potential benefits. However, consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your diet or treatment plan.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis tea, also known as dong quai, is a traditional Chinese herbal remedy that may help ease the symptoms of osteoarthritis. The tea is made from the roots of the Angelica sinensis plant, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These compounds can help reduce inflammation and pain in the joints, making it easier to move and perform daily activities. The tea may also help improve blood flow to the affected areas, which can promote healing and reduce stiffness in the joints. Additionally, the tea contains compounds that have been shown to have anti-cancer properties and may help protect against cell damage. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties in Angelica sinensis tea may also help reduce the severity of osteoarthritis symptoms, such as joint pain and swelling, and improve joint mobility and flexibility. Drinking Angelica sinensis tea regularly may also help reduce the risk of developing osteoarthritis in the first place, by protecting against cell damage and promoting healthy joint function.

Ligustilide is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling by blocking the production of chemicals that cause inflammation in the body.

If using fresh peppers, dry them in a low-temperature oven or using a food dehydrator to remove excess moisture. Next, combine 1-2 teaspoons of the dried pepper pods with 1 cup of boiling water in a teapot or a heat-resistant cup. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, which will help release the active compounds, including capsaicin. Once steeped, strain the liquid into a separate cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth, and discard the solids. You can add honey or lemon juice to taste, but be aware that the capsaicin may irritate your mouth and throat. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce pain and inflammation associated with osteoarthritis, but consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata tea has been used for centuries to help people with osteoarthritis. This is because it contains a compound called boswellic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink Boswellia serrata tea, the boswellic acid in it travels to your joints and helps reduce inflammation and pain. This is especially helpful for people with osteoarthritis, a condition where the cartilage in your joints breaks down and causes pain and stiffness. By reducing inflammation and pain, Boswellia serrata tea can help you move more easily and comfortably, and even improve your sleep. Additionally, Boswellia serrata tea may also help slow down the progression of osteoarthritis, giving you more time to enjoy your daily activities without pain. It's also a natural remedy, which means it has fewer side effects compared to some prescription medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Boswellic acids are good for osteoarthritis because they reduce inflammation and prevent cartilage breakdown, making it a potential natural remedy for people suffering from joint pain.

Terpenoids are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and inflammation, making it a potential natural remedy for people suffering from this condition.
Polyphenols are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and inflammation, and also promote the production of healthy cartilage.

Acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid is good for osteoarthritis because it can reduce inflammation and pain in joints by blocking the production of inflammatory enzymes, which can help to slow down the progression of the disease.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal teas that help with osteoarthritis, start by harvesting fresh Capsicum annuum leaves, also known as cayenne pepper or sweet pepper leaves. Clean the leaves thoroughly and chop them into small pieces. You will need about one cup of chopped leaves for every liter of water you plan to use. Next, boil the water in a pot and add the chopped Capsicum annuum leaves to the boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let the leaves steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the tea into a separate container and discard the leaves. You can drink the tea hot or cold, sweetened with honey if desired. The Capsaicin present in the leaves is known to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with osteoarthritis, providing relief and comfort to those suffering from the condition.

remedies.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum tea, made from the sweet pepper plant, may help with osteoarthritis pain. The capsaicin in this tea acts as a natural pain reliever, reducing inflammation in the joints. This can help ease pain and stiffness in people with osteoarthritis. Capsaicin also helps slow down the breakdown of cartilage, the protective tissue in joints, which can wear down and cause pain in people with osteoarthritis. Additionally, capsaicin has antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress in the body, a factor that contributes to joint damage. By drinking Capsicum annuum tea, people with osteoarthritis may be able to manage their symptoms and improve their quality of life.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Capsaicin is good for osteoarthritis because it helps reduce pain and inflammation by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which sends pain signals to the brain.
Quercetin is good for osteoarthritis because it helps reduce joint inflammation and pain by blocking the production of enzymes that cause inflammation and damage to the joints.

Ferulic acid is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and swelling, making it a potential natural remedy for people suffering from this condition.

Ellagic acid is good for osteoarthritis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and inflammation, and also has antioxidant properties that protect the joints from damage and promote healthy cartilage growth.
Tannins are good for osteoarthritis because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce joint pain and inflammation, allowing for easier movement and improved quality of life.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Capsicum annuum herbal teas that help with osteoarthritis, start by gathering fresh or dried Capsicum annuum leaves. If using fresh leaves, rinse them with clean water, then chop them into small pieces. If using dried leaves, skip this step. Next, boil one liter of water and add one teaspoon of chopped or dried Capsicum annuum leaves to the pot. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Add honey or sugar to taste, as the Capsicum annuum tea may have a slightly bitter flavor. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate osteoarthritis symptoms, such as joint pain and inflammation, by reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation.

supplementary_best: For osteoarthritis, a combination of Boswellia serrata, Curcuma longa, and Ginger (Zingiber officinale) is effective. Boswellia serrata has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce joint pain and swelling. Curcuma longa, or turmeric, has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help prevent joint damage and promote healing. Ginger has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help reduce pain and stiffness. This combination works well together because Boswellia serrata can help reduce inflammation, while Curcuma longa can help repair damaged joint tissue, and Ginger can help alleviate pain. When consumed together, these herbs can provide relief from osteoarthritis symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have osteoarthritis, there are some herbal teas you should avoid. One example is tea made from goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), which can slow down blood clotting and make bleeding more likely. If you're taking medications for osteoarthritis that also slow down blood clotting, combining them with goldenseal tea can increase the risk of bleeding. Another herb to avoid is tea made from comfrey (Symphytum officinale), which contains compounds that can cause liver damage and kidney problems. Drinking comfrey tea regularly can lead to these issues, especially if you already have liver or kidney problems. Tea made from pennyroyal (Mentha pulegium) is also not recommended, as it can cause liver damage and interact with certain medications. You should also avoid tea made from sassafras (Sassafras albidum), which contains a compound that can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding when combined with certain medications. Finally, tea made from wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) can cause stomach problems, including nausea and vomiting, which can be especially problematic for people with osteoarthritis who may already be experiencing joint pain and discomfort.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people with osteoarthritis, there are several natural remedies that can help. One of these is glucosamine, a compound found in cartilage that can help repair and rebuild damaged joints. When taken as a supplement, glucosamine has been shown to reduce joint pain and stiffness in people with osteoarthritis. Another option is omega-3 fatty acids, which are anti-inflammatory and can help reduce swelling and pain in the joints. Omega-3s can be found in foods such as salmon, flaxseed, and walnuts, or taken as a supplement. Turmeric, a spice commonly used in cooking, also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to have the same pain-relieving effects as some prescription medications. Another natural remedy that can help with osteoarthritis is MSM, or methylsulfonylmethane, a compound that can help reduce joint pain and inflammation. MSM can be found in foods such as garlic, onions, and cruciferous vegetables, or taken as a supplement. These natural remedies can be a useful addition to a treatment plan for people with osteoarthritis, and may be especially helpful for those who are looking for a more natural approach to managing their symptoms.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with osteoarthritis often experience other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. One common problem is chronic pain, which can be relieved with herbs like turmeric and ginger. These herbs contain compounds that reduce inflammation and ease pain. Turmeric has curcumin, which blocks pain-causing chemicals in the body, while ginger has a compound called gingerol that numbs the pain.  Many osteoarthritis sufferers also experience inflammation in their joints, which can be reduced with herbs like boswellia and willow bark. Boswellia has a compound called boswellic acid that stops inflammation-causing chemicals from being made, while willow bark contains salicin, a natural version of aspirin that reduces inflammation.  In addition, people with osteoarthritis may experience digestive problems, like bloating and constipation, due to poor gut health. Herbs like peppermint and dandelion root can help with digestion. Peppermint relaxes the muscles in the digestive system, making it easier to pass food through, while dandelion root stimulates digestion and helps to clear out waste.  Some osteoarthritis patients may also experience fatigue and low energy, which can be improved with herbs like ashwagandha and ginseng. These herbs boost energy levels by reducing stress and improving blood flow. Ashwagandha has adaptogenic properties that help the body cope with stress, while ginseng increases oxygen delivery to the cells, giving more energy.  Finally, people with osteoarthritis may experience mood changes, like anxiety and depression, due to chronic pain and stress. Herbs like St. John's Wort and passionflower can help with mood. St. John's Wort has compounds that reduce stress and anxiety, while passionflower has a sedative effect that calms the mind and body.
